jokes.text: I pulled a few boners
jokes.setup: Homer trying to give sage fatherly advice about mistakes
jokes.punchline: Uses 'boners' (slang for mistakes) which has sexual double meaning
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Homer's oblivious character - he doesn't realize the double meaning

jokes.text: I can't let my only boy get ripped limb from limb by a bloodthirsty mob
jokes.setup: Father-son bonding moment in dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact description of gruesome death while being sentimental
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixed with genuine parental sentiment - classic Simpsons tone

jokes.text: We'll die together, like a father and son should
jokes.setup: Expected: 'live together like father and son should'
jokes.punchline: Homer treats dying together as a normal family bonding activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expected father-son sentiment with absurd logic delivered earnestly

jokes.text: About 23 minutes and 5 seconds
jokes.setup: Mob asks how long his story will take
jokes.punchline: Homer gives the exact runtime of a TV episode
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons meta-humor breaking the fourth wall with precision timing

jokes.text: 23:05? Well, all right. Go ahead
jokes.setup: Bloodthirsty mob hearing the story length
jokes.punchline: They calmly agree to wait the exact episode length
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the meta joke, absurd politeness from an angry mob

jokes.text: Get your butts down here right now!
jokes.setup: Marge calling kids for church
jokes.punchline: Uses crude language ('butts') while preparing for religious service
jokes.explanation: Mild irony of using mildly inappropriate language before church

jokes.text: Bart, assume the position
jokes.setup: Sounds like police arrest command
jokes.punchline: Actually about clothing inspection for church
jokes.explanation: Misdirection joke using police/military terminology for domestic routine

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer watching football while family waits for church
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - not original here but perfect character integration and economy

jokes.text: This isn't gambling, Marge. It's a lead-pipe cinch
jokes.setup: Homer defending his bet to Marge
jokes.punchline: Claims it's not gambling because he's sure he'll win
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - terrible reasoning delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Laza who?
jokes.setup: TV announcer references Lazarus rising from the dead
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't recognize the biblical reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing obvious cultural reference while supposedly Christian

jokes.text: Whoa! Are you talking to me?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to get Bart's attention
jokes.punchline: Bart was wearing headphones, completely oblivious
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup - predictable but functional

jokes.text: Will there be cavemen in heaven?
jokes.setup: Sunday school Q&A about heaven
jokes.punchline: Random, illogical question about prehistoric humans
jokes.explanation: Captures child-like random curiosity mixed with Bart's mischievous nature

jokes.text: What if you're a really good person, but you're in a really, really bad fight and your leg gets gangrene, and it has to be amputated. Will it be waiting for you in heaven?
jokes.setup: Simple question about heaven
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and gruesome hypothetical scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of child logic - starts innocent, becomes disturbingly specific

jokes.text: Gambling, the Eighth Deadly Sin
jokes.setup: Sermon title about gambling
jokes.punchline: There are only seven deadly sins - he's made up an eighth
jokes.explanation: Subtle religious humor - preacher gets basic theology wrong

jokes.text: You can't get enough... of that wonderful Duff
jokes.setup: Beer commercial during football game
jokes.punchline: Duff beer slogan parody
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons brand parody - Duff as generic beer brand satire

jokes.text: It's... good to see you all in church
jokes.setup: Homer shouting about football kick being good
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly tries to cover by pretending he meant the congregation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic recovery attempt - Homer's desperate improvisation in embarrassing moment

jokes.text: What about a robot with a human brain?
jokes.setup: Sunday school discussion about heaven
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous sci-fi hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Escalation from previous heaven questions into science fiction territory

jokes.text: Is a little blind faith too much to ask?
jokes.setup: Teacher overwhelmed by Bart's theological questions
jokes.punchline: Frustrated admission that religion can't answer logical questions
jokes.explanation: Realistic teacher frustration with child who asks too many questions

jokes.text: He didn't say that under certain circumstances it was all right, did he?
jokes.setup: Homer asks about gambling sermon he missed
jokes.punchline: Desperately hoping for loophole in anti-gambling message
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wishful thinking - looking for moral loopholes

jokes.text: Those cute little monkeys? That's terrible
jokes.setup: Lisa says apes can't get into heaven
jokes.punchline: Marge more upset about monkey exclusion than theological implications
jokes.explanation: Sweet Marge character moment - focusing on emotional rather than theological aspects

jokes.text: What about those really smart ones who live among us, who roller-skate and smoke cigars?
jokes.setup: Concern about monkeys getting into heaven
jokes.punchline: Specific image of anthropomorphic circus monkeys
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurd image that escalates the monkey concern

jokes.text: Perish the thought
jokes.setup: Homer warns Bart about not seeing Space Mutants movie
jokes.punchline: Bart uses overly formal language while clearly planning to do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Bart's mock-innocent response with sophisticated vocabulary

jokes.text: Share the wealth. That's what I always say
jokes.setup: Homer gives Bart money
jokes.punchline: Ironic catchphrase from someone who just lost $50 gambling
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious generosity despite financial irresponsibility

jokes.text: Might land on my face and end up looking like you
jokes.setup: Jimbo asks Bart to repeat his skateboard trick
jokes.punchline: Bart's clever insult about Jimbo's appearance
jokes.explanation: Smart comeback that shows Bart's wit can handle bullies

jokes.text: You're witty
jokes.setup: Expected: bully would be offended by insult
jokes.punchline: Jimbo appreciates being insulted, finds it clever
jokes.explanation: Subverts bully stereotype - enjoys clever wordplay even when targeted

jokes.text: Well, okay. I just wanted to make sure we weren't deluding ourselves
jokes.setup: Bart expressing concern about sneaking into movies
jokes.punchline: Accepts theft after philosophical confirmation
jokes.explanation: Bart's pseudo-intellectual approach to moral compromise

jokes.text: I've got eyes in the back of my head
jokes.setup: Store clerk warning kids not to steal
jokes.punchline: Common expression that kids might take literally
jokes.explanation: Standard joke setup - relies on visual payoff of kids testing the claim

jokes.text: Share the wealth. That's what I always say
jokes.setup: Bart covering for shoplifting friends
jokes.punchline: Uses Homer's phrase while enabling theft
jokes.explanation: Ironic callback - Bart uses Homer's generous phrase to enable crime

jokes.text: Five-finger discount, man
jokes.setup: Bart asks where they got merchandise
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for stealing (five fingers = hand)
jokes.explanation: Common slang expression - not particularly original or clever

jokes.text: Like that one over there looks just like a cherry bomb
jokes.setup: Looking at clouds
jokes.punchline: Sees violent imagery in innocent cloud formations
jokes.explanation: Sets up pattern of seeing violence in innocent things

jokes.text: It looks like a guy with a switchblade stuck in his back
jokes.setup: Cloud watching game
jokes.punchline: Escalates to more violent and specific imagery
jokes.explanation: Escalation of violent imagery in innocent activity

jokes.text: That one looks like a school bus going over a cliff in flames, with kids inside screaming
jokes.setup: Continuing cloud game
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and disturbing violent scenario
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation of dark humor - disturbing specificity makes it funny

jokes.text: I mean, without the head, of course
jokes.setup: Bart says cloud looks like Jebediah Springfield statue
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to beheading that he'll later commit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant foreshadowing - plants the idea that will drive the plot

jokes.text: Can you think of a better way for Daddy to spend his hard-won 50 bucks?
jokes.setup: Homer at bowling alley with baby
jokes.punchline: Calling gambling winnings 'hard-won' while shopping for luxury items
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - his twisted priorities and self-justification

jokes.text: The Stealth Bowler: The pins don't know what hit them
jokes.setup: Homer reading bowling ball description
jokes.punchline: Military stealth technology applied to bowling
jokes.explanation: Satire of over-marketing consumer products with military terminology

jokes.text: Being popular is the most important thing in the world
jokes.setup: Bart asks about importance of popularity
jokes.punchline: Homer gives terrible parenting advice with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided wisdom that directly causes the episode's problems

jokes.text: A boy without mischief is like a bowling ball without a liquid center
jokes.setup: Homer giving parenting philosophy
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical metaphor using bowling ball obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Homer wisdom - completely nonsensical but delivered with authority

jokes.text: Top of the world, Ma
jokes.setup: Marge asks if Bart is alright
jokes.punchline: Classic movie reference while clearly troubled
jokes.explanation: Film reference (White Heat) that contrasts with Bart's guilt

jokes.text: The Hammer of Thor: It will send your pins to Val-halla
jokes.setup: Another bowling ball description
jokes.punchline: Norse mythology applied to bowling
jokes.explanation: Similar to Stealth Bowler joke - absurd marketing language

jokes.text: If anyone has any information, please dial 'O' and ask for the police. That number again, 'O'
jokes.setup: Police chief giving information hotline
jokes.punchline: Incompetent police work - using operator instead of real number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - treats modern police work like 1950s

jokes.text: Is the Leaning Tower of Pizza just a statue?
jokes.setup: Marge defending importance of statues
jokes.punchline: Says 'Pizza' instead of 'Pisa'
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism - simple but effective character humor

jokes.text: A beer, please, and make sure there's a head on it
jokes.setup: Discussion about missing statue head
jokes.punchline: Beer foam 'head' reference during decapitation discussion
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay connecting beer terminology to the episode's crime

jokes.text: Yeah, that was just cloud talk, man
jokes.setup: Bart reminds bullies they wanted statue beheaded
jokes.punchline: Dismisses previous enthusiasm as meaningless
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to earlier scene while showing bully hypocrisy

jokes.text: My head
jokes.setup: Asked what's in the bag
jokes.punchline: Literal answer that sounds like expression of frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - could mean frustration or literal contents

jokes.text: You're not really talking to me. You're just my overactive imagination
jokes.setup: Bart talking to statue head
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his guilt-induced hallucination
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where character recognizes cartoon logic

jokes.text: Turn them in, and Krusty will send you a free slide whistle just like Sideshow Bob's
jokes.setup: Krusty asking for information about crime
jokes.punchline: Pathetic reward for serious crime information
jokes.explanation: Krusty's commercial nature - trivial reward for major crime tip

jokes.text: Turn them in, and Krusty will send you a free slide whistle just like Sideshow Bob's
jokes.setup: Krusty asking for information about vandal
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small reward for turning in criminal
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Krusty's cheap incentive program

jokes.text: That's 'B-A-R-E' hands
jokes.setup: Lisa telling story of Jebediah killing bear with bare hands
jokes.punchline: Spelling out homophone to clarify meaning
jokes.explanation: Lisa's pedantic nature shown through unnecessary clarification

jokes.text: Although modern historians recently uncovered evidence that the bear, in fact, probably killed him
jokes.setup: Heroic founding myth
jokes.punchline: Historical revisionism deflating the legend
jokes.explanation: Satirizes how historical 'facts' are often myths - very Simpsons approach

jokes.text: I love you, Smithers
jokes.setup: Beautiful sunrise moment
jokes.punchline: Burns declares love for his assistant in emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable relationship dynamic played for both sentiment and comedy

jokes.text: I love you, Smithers. The feeling is more than mutual, sir
jokes.setup: Emotional moment during sunrise
jokes.punchline: Smithers' overly enthusiastic response to Burns' affection
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment establishing Smithers' devotion to Burns

jokes.text: The feeling is more than mutual, sir
jokes.setup: Burns' declaration of love
jokes.punchline: Smithers' awkward professional response to personal moment
jokes.explanation: Smithers' professional language can't hide his personal feelings

jokes.text: Most lynch mobs aren't this nice
jokes.setup: Peaceful resolution with angry mob
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual reference to lynch mob violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered as matter-of-fact parenting advice - perfect Homer
